{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 2","old":"DEPTH = 1","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)

        block_layers: list[nn.Module] = [
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
        ]
        for _ in range(DEPTH):
            block_layers.append(ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM))
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(*block_layers)
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.blocks(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","old":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.blocks(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The baseline model is likely under-capacity for CIFAR-10 under a short fixed-step budget. Modestly increasing width and stacking an extra residual block should improve feature quality early while keeping the script simple and stable.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With the stronger model and budget-aligned schedule in place, switching from Adam to AdamW should make weight decay behave more cleanly. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under a fixed 256-step budget, the model may benefit from seeing more unique training examples rather than taking more small-batch updates. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The last change suggests this workload is strongly data-limited under fixed steps. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Learning-rate scaling has remained consistently beneficial after the batch-size increases. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The last step overshot the optimum and degraded validation loss, so the simplest corrective move is to return to the previously best learning rate. {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimization and batching settings now look much stronger than the original architecture. Adding one more residual block is a simple capacity increase that preserves the same overall design and may let the model use the improved training regime more effectively.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 2","old":"DEPTH = 3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"build_scheduler","new":"","old":"","op":"replace_function","source":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 20
    warmup_steps = max(1, total_steps // 16)
    cosine_steps = max(1, total_steps - warmup_steps)
    warmup = optim.lr_scheduler.LinearLR(optimizer, start_factor=0.2, end_factor=1.0, total_iters=warmup_steps)
    cosine = optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=cosine_steps)
    return optim.lr_scheduler.SequentialLR(optimizer, schedulers=[warmup, cosine], milestones=[warmup_steps])","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The deeper variant regressed, so this returns to the stronger depth while testing a better budget allocation. With a fairly aggressive learning rate on a large batch, a short warmup before cosine decay may stabilize the early updates and improve the fixed-step outcome.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The lower beta2 did not help, and the current setup may be somewhat underfitting under only 256 updates. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest gains so far came from increasing batch size, which suggests this fixed-step workload benefits from covering more examples per update.
